title: श्री दत्तात्रेय सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दत्तात्रेय सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
जटाधरं पाण्डुरङ्गं शूलहस्तं कृपानिधिम्।
सर्वरोगहरं देवं दत्तात्रेयमहं भजे॥

दिगम्बरं भस्मधारिं कमण्डलुविभूषितम्।
चतुर्भुजं त्रिनेत्रं च दत्तं ध्यायेत् सदा बुधः॥

॥ सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥
ॐ दत्तात्रेयो महायोगी योगीशो योगवल्लभः।
योगसिद्धिप्रदो देवो योगानन्दो दयानिधिः॥ १॥

त्रिमूर्तिरव्ययो विष्णुर्ब्रह्मा रुद्रश्च शाश्वतः।
अनसूयासुतो दत्तः अत्रिपुत्रो महाप्रभुः॥ २॥

दिगम्बरो महातेजाः भस्मोद्धूलितविग्रहः।
कमण्डलुधरो धीरो डमरुध्वनिनादकः॥ ३॥

शूलहस्तो गदापाणिः शङ्खचक्रसुशोभितः।
त्रिशूलधारी वरदो अभयङ्करकारकः॥ ४॥

चतुर्भुजः षड्भुजश्च अष्टभुजो दशायुधः।
त्रिनेत्रो भालचन्द्रश्च जटाजूटसमन्वितः॥ ५॥

सृष्टिकर्ता जगद्धाता स्थितिकर्ता प्रजापतिः।
संहारकारी रुद्रात्मा कालरूपी कलानिधिः॥ ६॥

ब्रह्मज्ञानी परं ज्योतिः सच्चिदानन्दविग्रहः।
परमात्मा परं ब्रह्म आत्मारामो विरागवान्॥ ७॥

सर्वज्ञः सर्वदृक् साक्षी सर्वव्यापी जनार्दनः।
सर्वभूतहिते रतः सर्वलोकसुखावहः॥ ८॥

अवधूतो महामौनी परमहंससंस्थितः।
श्वानसेवी गजारूढो वृषभध्वजवाहनः॥ ९॥

गुरुर्गुरुतमो ज्ञानं ज्ञानगम्यो गुणाकरः।
निर्गुणो गुणसम्पन्नो गुणातीतो गुणप्रियः॥ १०॥

अनघो विमलो नित्यो ध्यानगम्यो निरञ्जनः।
परिव्राजकशिरोमणिः अतिवर्णाश्रमी प्रभुः॥ ११॥

भक्तवत्सलो दयालुश्च शरण्यो भक्तरक्षकः।
आपद्बन्धुः कृपासिन्धुः दीनबन्धुर्जगद्गुरुः॥ १२॥

सर्वसिद्धिप्रदो नाथः सर्वसम्पत्करो हरः।
सर्वमङ्गलदो दाता सर्वकामफलप्रदः॥ १३॥

सर्वव्याधिविनाशी च सर्वदुःखनिवारकः।
सर्वपापहरो देवः सर्वभयापहारकः॥ १४॥

ब्रह्मविद्याविशारदो मुक्तिदो मोक्षदायकः।
योगक्षेमकरो दाता योगिहृत्पद्मवासकः॥ १५॥

आदिगुरुः सनातनो निर्विकल्पो निराश्रयः।
निर्द्वन्द्वो निर्ममो शान्तो निष्कलो निष्प्रपञ्चकः॥ १६॥

चिदानन्दमयो दत्तो गोरक्षः पशुपालकः।
जगत्त्रयगुरुर्देवो वेदवेदाङ्गपारगः॥ १७॥

सिद्धसंघसमाराध्यो मुनिवन्द्यो महायशाः।
वाल्मीकिनारदादीनां गुरुः सत्यस्वरूपकः॥ १८॥

कामधेनुस्वरूपश्च कल्पवृक्षसमप्रभः।
चिन्तामणिर्गुणनिधिः अक्षयः शाश्वतोऽक्षरः॥ १९॥

सहस्रनामसम्पन्नो दत्तात्रेयो दयापरः।
त्रयीमयो वेदमूर्तिः भवभीतिविनाशकः॥ २०॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
दत्तात्रेयसहस्रनामस्तोत्रं यः पठेत् सदा।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो दत्तसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥

गुरुवारे विशेषेण पठनाद् ब्रह्मवर्चसम्।
योगसिद्धिं ज्ञानसिद्धिं मुक्तिसिद्धिं च विन्दति॥

॥ इति श्री मार्कण्डेयपुराणान्तर्गते श्री दत्तात्रेय सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dattatreya Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Jatadharam Pandurangam Shulahastam Kripanidhim.
Sarvarogaharam Devam Dattatreyamaham Bhaje.

Digambaram Bhasmadhaarim Kamandaluvibhushitam.
Chaturbhujam Trinetram Cha Dattam Dhyayet Sada Budhah.

|| Sahasranama Stotram ||
Om Dattatreyo Mahayogi Yogisho Yogavallabhah.
Yogasiddhiprado Devo Yoganando Dayanidhih. || 1 ||

Trimurti-ravyayo Vishnur-Brahma Rudrash-cha Shashvatah.
Anasuyasuto Dattah Atriputro Mahaprabhuh. || 2 ||

Digambaro Mahatejah Bhasmoddhulitavigrahah.
Kamandaludharo Dhiro Damarudhvaninadakah. || 3 ||

Shulahasto Gadapanih Shankhachakrasushobhitah.
Trishuldhari Varado Abhayankara-karakah. || 4 ||

Chaturbhujah Shadbhujash-cha Ashtabhujo Dashayudhah.
Trinetro Bhalachandrash-cha Jatajutasamanvitah. || 5 ||

Srishtikarta Jagaddhata Sthitikarta Prajapatih.
Samharakari Rudratma Kalarupi Kalanidhih. || 6 ||

Brahmajnani Param Jyotih Sachchidanandavigrahah.
Paramatma Param Brahma Atmaramo Viragavan. || 7 ||

Sarvajnah Sarvadrik Sakshi Sarvavyapi Janardanah.
Sarvabhutahite Ratah Sarvalokasukhaavahah. || 8 ||

Avadhuto Mahamauni Paramahamsasamsthitah.
Shvanasevi Gajarudho Vrishabhadhvajavaahanah. || 9 ||

Gurur-gurutamo Jnanam Jnanagamyo Gunakarah.
Nirguno Gunasampanno Gunatito Gunapriyah. || 10 ||

Anagho Vimalo Nityo Dhyanagamyo Niranjanah.
Parivrajakashiromanih Ativarnashrami Prabhuh. || 11 ||

Bhaktavatsalo Dayalush-cha Sharanyah Bhaktarakshakah.
Apadbandhuh Kripasindhuh Dinabandhur-jagadguruh. || 12 ||

Sarvasiddhiprado Nathah Sarvasampatkarao Harah.
Sarvamangalado Data Sarvakamaphalapradah. || 13 ||

Sarvavyadhi-vinashi Cha Sarvaduhkhanivarakah.
Sarvapapaharo Devah Sarvabhayapaharakah. || 14 ||

Brahmavidyavisharado Muktido Mokshadayakah.
Yogakshemakaro Data Yogihritpadmavasakah. || 15 ||

Adiguruh Sanatano Nirvikalpo Nirashrayah.
Nirdvandvo Nirmamo Shanto Nishkalo Nishprapanchakah. || 16 ||

Chidanandamayo Datto Gorakshah Pashupalakah.
Jagattrayagurur-devo Vedavedangaparagah. || 17 ||

Siddhasanghasamaradhyo Munivandyo Mahayashah.
ValmikiNaradaadinam Guruh Satyaswarupakah. || 18 ||

KamadhenuSwarupash-cha Kalpavrikshasamaprabhah.
Chintamanir-gunanidhih Akshayah Shashvato-aksharah. || 19 ||

Sahasranamasampanno Dattatreyo Dayaparah.
Trayeemayo Vedamurtih Bhavabhitivinashakah. || 20 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Dattatreya-sahasranama-stotram Yah Pathet Sada.
Sarvapapavinirmukto Dattasayujyamaapnuyat.

Guruvare Visheshena Pathanad Brahmavarchasam.
Yogasiddhim Jnanasiddhim Muktisiddhim Cha Vindati.

|| Iti Shri Markandeyapuranantargate Shri Dattatreya Sahasranama Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: जटाधारी, पाण्डुरंग (गौर वर्ण), शूल हस्त, कृपानिधि, सर्वरोगहर भगवान दत्तात्रेय को भजता हूँ। दिगम्बर, भस्मधारी, कमण्डलुधारी, चतुर्भुज, त्रिनेत्र दत्त का सदा ध्यान करें।

१-५: दत्तात्रेय महायोगी, योगीश, त्रिमूर्ति (ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-रुद्र स्वरूप), अनसूया-अत्रि के पुत्र, दिगम्बर, भस्मधारी, शूलहस्त, त्रिशूलधारी, चतुर्भुज-षड्भुज-अष्टभुज रूप, त्रिनेत्र, जटाजूटधारी हैं।

६-१०: सृष्टिकर्ता, स्थितिकर्ता, संहारकारी, ब्रह्मज्ञानी, सच्चिदानन्द विग्रह, परमात्मा, अवधूत, परमहंस, गुरुतम, निर्गुण और गुणातीत हैं।

११-१५: अनघ, निरञ्जन, भक्तवत्सल, आपद्बन्धु, कृपासिन्धु, जगद्गुरु, सर्वसिद्धिप्रद, सर्वव्याधिविनाशी, सर्वपापहर, ब्रह्मविद्याविशारद, मोक्षदायक हैं।

१६-२०: आदिगुरु, सनातन, निर्विकल्प, चिदानन्दमय, गोरक्षक, कामधेनुस्वरूप, कल्पवृक्षसम, चिन्तामणि, भवभीतिविनाशक हैं।

फलश्रुति: जो इस सहस्रनाम का सदा पाठ करता है वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर दत्त सायुज्य प्राप्त करता है। गुरुवार को विशेष पाठ से ब्रह्मवर्चस, योगसिद्धि, ज्ञानसिद्धि और मुक्तिसिद्धि मिलती है।
benefits: Contains the thousand divine names of Lord Dattatreya (Trimurti incarnation)
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants datta sayujya (union with dattatreya)
Invokes the blessing of yoga siddhi, jnana siddhi, and mukti siddhi
Invokes brahma varchas (divine spiritual radiance) on thursday recitation
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases, sorrows, and fears through dattatreya's grace
description: Dattatreya Sahasranama Stotram (श्री दत्तात्रेय सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names dedicated to Lord Dattatreya. Rooted in Markandeya Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow Yoga Siddhi, Jnana Siddhi, and Mukti Siddhi and remove all diseases, sorrows, and fears through Dattatreya's grace. It is also said to contain the thousand divine names of Lord Dattatreya (Trimurti incarnation). This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Datta Jayanti and Guru Purnima to invoke divine grace. The text contains 20 verses and takes around 25 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Dattatreya Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Dattatreya Sahasranama in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Lord Dattatreya for spiritual growth, yoga siddhi, and liberation.
relatedSearches: Dattatreya Sahasranama lyrics in Sanskrit PDF
Trimurti Brahma Vishnu Mahesh combined Dattatreya
Narasimha Saraswati Manik Prabhu Sai Baba Datta avatars
Twenty-four gurus of Dattatreya from nature

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dattatreya Sahasranama and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dattatreya Sahasranama Stotram is a Sanskrit hymn enumerating one thousand names (sahasranama) of Lord Dattatreya, the Trimurti-incarnation born of sage Atri and Anasuya in whom Brahma, Vishnu and Mahesh manifest in a single body. The most widely recited version is preserved in the Brahmanda Purana and reproduced in the Datta-prabhakara, the Datta-mahatmya, and the Sripada Srivallabha Charitamrita. The opening dhyana describes Dattatreya as jata-dharam (matted-locks), pandurangam (white-complexioned), shula-hastam (trident-bearing), kripanidhim (treasure of compassion), the universal healer of all diseases. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; Datta-mahatmya; Stotra Nidhi Datta texts.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Dattatreya and what is his significance in Hindu tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Dattatreya is the unique deity in whom Brahma, Vishnu and Mahesh combine into a single three-headed, six-armed form, born of sage Atri and his chaste wife Anasuya as told in the Markandeya, Bhagavata and Brahmanda Puranas. The Trimurti tested Anasuya's chastity by appearing as guests demanding alms in nakedness; she sprinkled water from her husband's feet, transforming them into infants, and on Atri's request the trio merged and was 'given' (datta) to the couple as their son — hence Datta-atreya (given to Atri). He is the adi-guru of the Avadhuta tradition, mentioned as guru of King Yadu in the Bhagavata's twenty-four gurus chapter, and the patron deity of the Mahanubhava, Datta Sampradaya, and Nath traditions. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 11; Markandeya Purana; Wikipedia Dattatreya; Hindupedia Datta.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Dattatreya described iconographically in this sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sahasranama opens with epithets describing Dattatreya as digambara (sky-clad, ascetic), bhasma-dharin (smeared with sacred ash), kamandalu-vibhushita (adorned with the water-pot), chaturbhuja or shadbhuja (four or six-armed), trinetra (three-eyed), trimukha (three-faced — Brahma, Vishnu, Mahesh facing different directions), holding shankha, chakra, trishula, damaru, japa-mala and kamandalu. He is accompanied by four dogs (representing the four Vedas) and a cow (representing the Earth and dharma). He is white-complexioned, seated under the audumbara (cluster fig) tree which is sacred to him, and is described as ever-young, compassionate and instantly responsive to devotees. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Datta dhyana; Datta-prabhakara; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Dattatreya Sahasranama be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday is sacred to Dattatreya across Marathi, Telugu and Kannada traditions. The grandest occasion is Datta Jayanti, the Purnima of Margashirsha (December full moon), when the Sahasranama is recited continuously at Datta peethas like Ganagapur, Audumbar, Narasimhawadi, Pithapuram and Mahur. Devotees also recite it on every Thursday, Krishna Ashtami, the entire Margashirsha month, and during the seven-day Datta saptaha (a parayana cycle of seven days). Daily recitation in the early morning brahma-muhurta is recommended for those seeking guru-grace, healing of chronic illness (Dattatreya being sarva-roga-hara), and removal of major life obstacles. (Sources: Drikpanchang Datta Jayanti; Datta-mahatmya parayana vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Dattatreya Sahasranama promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares the Sahasranama grants sarva-roga-shanti (cure of all diseases — Datta is invoked as sarva-roga-hara), removal of bhuta-preta-pisacha-baadha, mitigation of grievous karma-doshas, attainment of guru, worldly prosperity together with spiritual liberation (yoga-kshema), protection during travel, removal of legal complications, and ultimately moksha through identification with the Avadhuta-tattva. Datta-bhaktas hold that sincere recitation invariably brings the guru — either Datta himself in disguise or a true human guru — into the chanter's life within forty mandalas. The Sahasranama is the principal Datta upasana for householders unable to perform extended sadhana. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phalashruti; Datta-mahatmya; Sripada Srivallabha Charitamrita.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between Dattatreya and his many avatars in the Datta Sampradaya?
seoFaqs.answer: The Datta Sampradaya recognises a continuing line of avatars in whom Dattatreya manifests in successive ages. The two foundational avatars accepted across all sub-traditions are Sripada Srivallabha (1320-1350 CE, Pithapuram-Kuravapuram) and Narasimha Saraswati (1378-1458 CE, Karanja-Ganagapur). Later widely-accepted Datta avatars include Manik Prabhu (Humnabad), Akkalkot Swami Samartha, Vasudevananda Saraswati (Tembe Swami), and many devotees regard Sai Baba of Shirdi as a Datta-rupa. The Dattatreya Sahasranama is recited at the samadhi-shrines of all these avatars, and reading the Sripada Srivallabha Charitamrita and Gurucharitra is recommended alongside the Sahasranama parayana. (Sources: Sripada Srivallabha Charitamrita; Gurucharitra of Saraswati Gangadhara; Hindupedia Datta Sampradaya.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Dattatreya Sahasranama related to the Datta Bavani and Avadhuta Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: The three texts form the core Datta liturgical canon. The Dattatreya Sahasranama is the formal one-thousand-name hymn for elaborate Thursday and Datta Jayanti puja. The Datta Bavani is the much shorter fifty-two verse Marathi devotional composed by Sant Rang Avadhut, recited daily by Maharashtrian Datta-bhaktas and held to grant immediate protection. The Avadhuta Gita is the philosophical text spoken by Dattatreya himself in twenty-four chapters, expounding non-dual Avadhuta realisation. Many devotees recite the Datta Bavani daily, the Sahasranama on Thursdays and Datta Jayanti, and study the Avadhuta Gita as the meditative culmination of Datta sadhana. (Sources: Datta Bavani by Rang Avadhut; Avadhuta Gita translations; Hindupedia Datta texts.)
